DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
accessibility artifact-reference example extensions guideline link metadata rich-text status usecase
anatomy api component design-specifications state variant
token-api token-value token
style
pattern

DSDS Style Definitions

Definitions for documenting macro-level visual style guidelines — color palettes, typography systems, spacing scales, elevation levels, and other visual attributes. Style documentation provides the rationale, principles, and usage rules that govern how token values are applied across the system.

Source: style/style.schema.json

4 definitions in this file:

  • principle
  • scaleStep
  • scale
  • styleDoc

principle

A high-level guiding principle for the visual style.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
titlestringrequiredA short name for the principle.
descriptionrichTextrequired

References: richText

Example

[
  {
    "title": "Use the scale",
    "description": "Every spacing value in production code must reference a token from the spacing scale. Hard-coded pixel, rem, or em values are not permitted. The scale exists to constrain decisions to a set of values that are proven to produce consistent rhythm."
  },
  {
    "title": "Density over decoration",
    "description": "Spacing exists to create clear relationships between elements — not to fill empty areas. Tighter spacing signals that elements are related. Wider spacing signals separation. Choose spacing based on the relationship between elements, not on what looks good in isolation."
  },
  {
    "title": "Accessible by default",
    "description": "All color combinations in the system meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast requirements. AAA compliance is the goal for body text. Token pairings are pre-validated at the system level so that individual component authors do not need to manually verify contrast."
  },
  {
    "title": "Functional first",
    "description": "Color choices serve functional needs — status, hierarchy, interaction — before aesthetic ones. Every color in the system has a semantic purpose. Decorative use of color is secondary to its communicative role."
  }
]

scaleStep

A single step in an ordered scale (e.g., a type size, a spacing value, an elevation level).

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
tokenstringrequiredThe token name for this step. MUST reference a token defined in the token schema.
labelstringoptionalA human-readable label (e.g., 'sm', 'md', 'lg', '2xl').
valuestringoptionalThe resolved value for display (e.g., '16px', '#1a1a1a', '400').
descriptionrichTextoptional

References: richText

Example

[
  {
    "token": "space-0",
    "label": "0",
    "value": "0px",
    "description": "Zero spacing. Use when two elements must share an edge. Always declare explicitly to communicate intent."
  },
  {
    "token": "space-1",
    "label": "4xs",
    "value": "2px",
    "description": "Minimal spacing. Use for tight optical adjustments, such as aligning an icon to text baseline."
  },
  {
    "token": "space-2",
    "label": "3xs",
    "value": "4px",
    "description": "Extra-small spacing. Use for the gap between an icon and its adjacent label, or between tightly coupled inline elements."
  },
  {
    "token": "space-3",
    "label": "2xs",
    "value": "8px",
    "description": "Small spacing. Use for internal padding in compact components (badges, chips, tags) and the gap between a form label and its input."
  },
  {
    "token": "space-4",
    "label": "xs",
    "value": "12px",
    "description": "Small-medium spacing. Use for internal padding in medium-density components and the gap between related form fields."
  },
  {
    "token": "space-5",
    "label": "sm",
    "value": "16px",
    "description": "Base spacing. The default for internal component padding (buttons, inputs, cards) and the gap between sibling elements in a standard layout."
  },
  {
    "token": "space-6",
    "label": "md",
    "value": "24px",
    "description": "Large spacing. Use for padding inside containers (cards, dialogs, panels) and the gap between distinct content groups within a surface."
  },
  {
    "token": "space-7",
    "label": "lg",
    "value": "32px",
    "description": "Extra-large spacing. Use for the gap between sections within a page or between unrelated content groups."
  },
  {
    "token": "space-8",
    "label": "xl",
    "value": "48px",
    "description": "Double extra-large spacing. Use for major section breaks on desktop layouts."
  }
]

scale

An ordered sequence of values that forms a visual scale (e.g., type scale, spacing scale, elevation scale). Each step references a design token.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
namestringrequiredMachine-readable name of the scale.
descriptionrichTextrequired
stepsscaleStep[]requiredThe ordered steps in the scale, from smallest/lowest to largest/highest.
Min items: 1
displayNamestringoptionalHuman-readable name.

References: richText, scaleStep

Example

{
  "name": "spacing-scale",
  "displayName": "Spacing Scale",
  "description": "A geometric spacing scale built on a 4px base unit. Values double at regular intervals to produce a natural rhythm. Use these tokens for all padding, margin, and gap properties. The scale is intentionally constrained — if a needed value is not on the scale, reconsider the design before requesting a new token.",
  "steps": [
    {
      "token": "space-0",
      "label": "0",
      "value": "0px"
    },
    {
      "token": "space-1",
      "label": "4xs",
      "value": "2px"
    },
    {
      "token": "space-2",
      "label": "3xs",
      "value": "4px"
    },
    {
      "token": "space-3",
      "label": "2xs",
      "value": "8px"
    },
    {
      "token": "space-4",
      "label": "xs",
      "value": "12px"
    },
    {
      "token": "space-5",
      "label": "sm",
      "value": "16px",
      "description": "The base unit. Use for default internal component padding."
    },
    {
      "token": "space-6",
      "label": "md",
      "value": "24px"
    },
    {
      "token": "space-7",
      "label": "lg",
      "value": "32px"
    },
    {
      "token": "space-8",
      "label": "xl",
      "value": "48px"
    }
  ]
}

styleDoc

Documentation for a macro-level visual style — the principles, guidelines, scales, and token references that govern a visual attribute such as color, typography, spacing, or elevation across the entire system.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
namestringrequiredMachine-readable identifier (e.g., 'color', 'typography', 'spacing', 'elevation').
Pattern: ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$
displayNamestringrequiredHuman-readable name.
descriptionrichTextrequired
statusstatusrequired
categorystringrequiredThe visual attribute category (e.g., 'color', 'typography', 'spacing', 'elevation', 'motion', 'shape').
summarystringoptionalA one-line, plain-text summary. MUST NOT contain markup.
deprecationNoticestringoptionalRequired when status is 'deprecated'.
sincestringoptionalThe version in which this style was introduced.
tagsstring[]optionalFreeform keywords to relate guidelines across categories/disciplines (e.g., 'rtl', 'localization', 'validation').
principlesprinciple[]optionalHigh-level guiding principles for this visual style.
tokenGroupsartifactReference[]optionalReferences to the token groups that implement this style. Connects macro-level guidance to concrete token values defined in the token schema.
scalesscale[]optionalOrdered scales that define the progression of values for this style (e.g., a type scale, a spacing scale, an elevation scale). Each scale step references a design token.
useCasesuseCasesoptional
guidelinesguideline[]optionalUsage guidelines for the style. Each entry is a self-contained guideline. Use the `category` property on individual guidelines to group by concern (e.g., 'design', 'accessibility', 'content').
accessibilityaccessibilityObjectoptional
linkslink[]optionalExternal resource links and related artifact references.
$extensionsextensionsoptional

Conditional: When status is "deprecated", then deprecationNotice is required.

References: richText, status, principle, artifactReference, scale, useCases, guideline, accessibilityObject, link, extensions

Example

{
  "name": "spacing",
  "displayName": "Spacing",
  "description": "A spatial system built on a 4px base unit. Defines the scale and rules for all whitespace, padding, margin, and gap values across the system. Eliminates ad hoc pixel values and reduces visual inconsistency caused by individual interpretation of spatial relationships.",
  "status": "stable",
  "since": "1.0.0",
  "category": "spacing",
  "summary": "A constrained spacing scale built on a 4px base unit.",
  "tags": [
    "layout",
    "spatial",
    "whitespace",
    "padding",
    "margin",
    "gap"
  ],
  "principles": [
    {
      "title": "Use the scale",
      "description": "Every spacing value must reference a token from the spacing scale. No hard-coded values."
    },
    {
      "title": "Density over decoration",
      "description": "Spacing exists to create clear relationships between elements, not to fill empty areas."
    },
    {
      "title": "Consistent internal, flexible external",
      "description": "Internal spacing within a component is fixed by the component's design specification. External spacing between components is determined by the layout context."
    }
  ],
  "tokenGroups": [
    {
      "name": "spacing",
      "role": "Provides all spatial tokens from space-0 (0px) through space-8 (48px), built on a 4px base unit."
    }
  ],
  "scales": [
    {
      "name": "spacing-scale",
      "displayName": "Spacing Scale",
      "description": "A geometric spacing scale based on a 4px unit.",
      "steps": [
        {
          "token": "space-0",
          "label": "0",
          "value": "0px"
        },
        {
          "token": "space-1",
          "label": "4xs",
          "value": "2px"
        },
        {
          "token": "space-2",
          "label": "3xs",
          "value": "4px"
        },
        {
          "token": "space-3",
          "label": "2xs",
          "value": "8px"
        },
        {
          "token": "space-4",
          "label": "xs",
          "value": "12px"
        },
        {
          "token": "space-5",
          "label": "sm",
          "value": "16px"
        },
        {
          "token": "space-6",
          "label": "md",
          "value": "24px"
        },
        {
          "token": "space-7",
          "label": "lg",
          "value": "32px"
        },
        {
          "token": "space-8",
          "label": "xl",
          "value": "48px"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "useCases": {
    "whenToUse": [
      {
        "description": "When defining padding, margin, or gap values for any layout or component. All spatial values in production code must reference the spacing scale."
      },
      {
        "description": "When establishing vertical rhythm between content sections, form groups, or card layouts."
      },
      {
        "description": "When setting internal padding for containers such as cards, dialogs, panels, and page regions."
      },
      {
        "description": "When controlling the gap between sibling elements in Flexbox or Grid layouts."
      }
    ],
    "whenNotToUse": [
      {
        "description": "When defining border widths, outline offsets, or stroke values.",
        "alternative": {
          "name": "border-width",
          "rationale": "Border tokens are tuned for visual weight at sub-pixel and single-pixel sizes. Spacing tokens start at 0px and jump to values optimized for whitespace, which produce incorrect visual results when applied to borders."
        }
      },
      {
        "description": "When setting font sizes or line heights.",
        "alternative": {
          "name": "typography",
          "rationale": "Typographic tokens follow a modular scale designed for readability and vertical rhythm. Spacing tokens follow a geometric scale designed for whitespace. The two scales serve different purposes and should not be interchanged."
        }
      },
      {
        "description": "When sizing icons or illustration containers.",
        "alternative": {
          "name": "icon-size",
          "rationale": "Icon size tokens are calibrated to optical alignment with adjacent text at each type scale step. Spacing tokens do not account for optical sizing and produce misaligned icons."
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "guidelines": [
    {
      "guidance": "Use the spacing scale tokens for all padding, margin, and gap properties. Do not use hard-coded values.",
      "rationale": "Hard-coded values create visual inconsistency and are not responsive to system-wide spacing changes. Tokens enable global adjustment of spatial density from a single source of truth.",
      "type": "required",
      "category": "visual-design"
    },
    {
      "guidance": "Components must not apply external margin. Margin between components is the responsibility of the parent layout.",
      "rationale": "Components that own their margins create unpredictable spacing when composed. A button with built-in margin-bottom behaves differently depending on what follows it. Delegating margin to layout makes spacing composable and predictable.",
      "type": "required",
      "category": "visual-design"
    },
    {
      "guidance": "Select spacing based on the relationship between elements, not their size. Related elements use smaller spacing. Unrelated elements use larger spacing.",
      "rationale": "Gestalt proximity principle: objects that are closer together are perceived as related. Spacing encodes information hierarchy. A label 4px from its input is clearly associated with it. A label 32px from an input appears disconnected.",
      "type": "encouraged",
      "category": "visual-design"
    },
    {
      "guidance": "Do not use spacing tokens for non-spatial properties such as border-width, font-size, or icon-size. Use the tokens designated for those properties.",
      "rationale": "Spacing tokens are tuned for whitespace. A spacing scale step that works well as padding produces incorrect results as a border-width or icon-size. Using the wrong token category couples unrelated visual properties.",
      "type": "prohibited",
      "category": "development"
    },
    {
      "guidance": "Use CSS gap (in Flexbox or Grid) as the primary mechanism for spacing between sibling elements. Reserve margin for spacing between non-sibling elements or layout-level offset.",
      "rationale": "Gap applies spacing uniformly between children without affecting the first or last child. Margin requires :first-child/:last-child overrides to prevent unwanted space at container edges.",
      "type": "encouraged",
      "category": "development"
    },
    {
      "guidance": "For responsive layouts, reduce spacing density at smaller breakpoints by stepping down the scale. Do not introduce arbitrary breakpoint-specific values.",
      "rationale": "Stepping down the scale (e.g., space-7 at desktop becoming space-5 at mobile) maintains proportional relationships while conserving space. Arbitrary values break out of the scale and undermine system consistency.",
      "type": "encouraged",
      "category": "visual-design"
    },
    {
      "guidance": "Ensure all interactive elements maintain a minimum 44x44 CSS pixel touch target, inclusive of padding.",
      "rationale": "Users with motor impairments and touch device users require a minimum target size to interact reliably. WCAG 2.5.8 requires 24x24px minimum and recommends 44x44px. The spacing system's role is to ensure padding contributes to meeting this target.",
      "type": "required",
      "category": "accessibility",
      "criteria": [
        "https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#target-size-minimum"
      ]
    },
    {
      "guidance": "When users increase text size to 200% via browser settings, spacing must not collapse to zero or cause content to overlap.",
      "rationale": "Users with low vision enlarge text. If spacing is defined in fixed pixel units that do not scale, text enlargement causes overlap and loss of content. Use rem-based spacing tokens where possible, and test all layouts at 200% text zoom.",
      "type": "required",
      "category": "accessibility",
      "criteria": [
        "https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#resize-text"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "accessibility": {
    "wcagLevel": "AA",
    "colorContrast": [],
    "keyboardInteraction": [],
    "ariaAttributes": []
  },
  "links": [
    {
      "type": "source",
      "url": "https://code.acme.com/design-system/src/tokens/spacing.tokens.json",
      "label": "Token source file"
    },
    {
      "type": "design",
      "url": "https://design-tool.acme.com/file/abc123?node-id=200:1",
      "label": "Design file — spacing variables"
    },
    {
      "type": "documentation",
      "url": "https://design.acme.com/style/spacing",
      "label": "Documentation site"
    },
    {
      "type": "related",
      "url": "https://design.acme.com/style/typography",
      "label": "Typography style"
    },
    {
      "type": "related",
      "url": "https://design.acme.com/components/stack",
      "label": "Stack component"
    }
  ]
}
↑ Back to top
